methods.description: This method involves the quantitative analysis of immunogold labelling to determine the density of gold particles in the cell wall. The analysis is performed using electron micrographs from labelling experiments, and the results are statistically tested.

steps.description: The density of gold labelling, expressed as the number of gold particles lm À2, was determined in the cell wall by counting the number of gold particles over the cell wall compartment.
steps.searchable_summary: count gold particles in cell wall from electron micrographs

steps.description: The number of gold particles was counted on randomly chosen electron micrographs obtained from three different labelling experiments for each sample, and three different samples were obtained for each plant system.
steps.searchable_summary: select random electron micrographs from experiments for counting

steps.description: The density of gold labelling was expressed as the average number of gold particles lm À2 6 standard error.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average density and standard error of gold particles

steps.description: The areas were estimated by a point-counting procedure, using a 15315 mm square grid.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate areas using point-counting with a grid

steps.description: The minimum sample size (MSS) was determined by the progressive mean technique.
steps.searchable_summary: determine minimum sample size using progressive mean technique

steps.description: All the quantitative analyses were carried out using a BASIC programme developed by Dr J Renau-Piqueras (La Fe Hospital Research Centre, Valencia, Spain) and the results represented in histograms.
steps.searchable_summary: perform quantitative analysis using BASIC program and represent results in histograms

steps.description: The results were statistically tested with a Student's t test where a=0.05.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Student's t test on results with alpha 0.05
